<|10.50|> We can forget about God at all times.<|13.16|>
<|13.78|> I can forget. We can all forget about God.<|17.00|>

<|18.56|> The perceived scarcity of the former and the promised abundance of the latter.<|24.60|>

<|14.68|> What kind of people do we seek to become?<|18.76|>
<|18.76|> What kind of world do we want to live in?<|20.40|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Our faith is in a world of thanksgiving, a world of gratitude, a world overcome with joy.<|10.80|>

<|17.28|> My help comes from the Lord, the maker of heaven and earth.<|21.82|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> to<|1.06|><|endoftext|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Yesterday, I received a phone call from a parishioner named Rebecca and her son, Max,<|7.94|>
<|8.38|> who had a Thanksgiving event on Tuesday evening for her work.<|12.52|>
<|14.52|> At the end of the meal, the volunteers didn't know what to do with all that leftover food<|19.72|>
<|19.72|> and were going to simply throw it away.<|21.58|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> But then something remarkable happened. Rebecca intervened and told them, no, don't. I will take it. I know there is someone who can use it.<|14.26|><|endoftext|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Without a plan, she told me, guided by faith, Rebecca loaded her car with trays of salad, sides, spices, and whole chickens and called our church.<|12.96|>
<|14.68|> Upon receiving her message, I contacted our neighbors across the street at the St. Francis Apartments.<|21.80|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> where people transitioning out of homelessness and being unhoused are offered subsidized housing and a second chance.<|8.96|>
<|11.10|> We were able to ensure that none of that harvest was forgotten,<|17.46|>
<|18.46|> but all of it, all of it put to good use.<|22.82|>
<|22.82|> We were able to ensure that none of that harvest was forgotten.<|23.70|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> To feed hungry souls and enlighten our spirit.<|4.66|>
<|7.08|> Know, know that this story of love is repeating itself here over and over again because of you.<|18.12|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.00|><|endoftext|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> This Thanksgiving, the dean, clergy, staff, and I are profoundly grateful for each and every one of you.<|17.40|><|endoftext|>

<|8.20|> and serve Christ in the light and dark places of our world,<|13.60|>
<|14.74|> for the infinite love that is among us,<|20.10|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And so it is with humble thanks that I ask that you are sent to remember God.<|10.50|>
<|12.82|> Remember to give thanks, to give thanks at all times and in all places, to<|20.30|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> To offer praise for the gifts you have received and the invitation to be generous.<|7.94|>
